metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend, Hackney families! I've found some fantastic Easter-themed activities like the Camden Market Easter Weekender and the Natural History Museum's Easter Family Festival. Plus, there are exciting theatre shows and museum explorations perfect for your little one.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed, free activity suitable for all ages up to 13, offering a calm space for reading.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Museum designed for children, with collections of toys, games, and childhood objects.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for children, with interactive exhibits and plenty to explore.

transport.tip: Avoid peak hours for a less crowded experience.

activities.title: Camden Market Easter Weekender
activities.description: Easter art trail, face painting, and bonnet making.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry and a variety of activities suitable for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Camden Road, then walk
transport.tip: Can get very crowded, so arrive early.

activities.title: Inside Aardman: Wallace & Gromit and Friends at Young V&A
activities.description: Explore how the iconic characters are brought to life. Ticket gives unlimited visits. From £11, u3 free.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and engaging for children, bringing beloved characters to life.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small, beautifully designed wooden playground with a pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for toddlers with its safe and engaging design.

transport.tip: Combine with a visit to the Olympic Park.

activities.title: Half Moon Theatre
activities.description: Presents professional theatre for children, with shows at the Limehouse venue and touring nationally.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a range of live shows and virtual performances suitable for young audiences.

transport.tip: Check their website for the schedule and age recommendations.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm for Easter crafts and animals
activities.description: Free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts, with a host of goats, sheep and smaller animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for animal lovers and offers hands-on activities.

activities.title: Weekly Story and Rhyme Time
activities.description: Stories and songs for babies and toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: A free, fun and social activity that encourages literacy.

transport.tip: Check the Hackney Council website for times and locations.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Search for giant Easter eggs hidden around the zoo and crack a secret password.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animal viewing with an engaging Easter-themed activity.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Camden Road, then bus
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance to save time.

activities.title: Natural History Museum Easter Family Festival
activities.description: Free family-friendly activities, talks, workshops, and Lego Super Builder sessions.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging and educational activities suitable for the whole family.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to South Kensington (District, Circle, Piccadilly lines)
transport.tip: Book in advance for popular workshops.

activities.title: The Science Museum
activities.description: Interactive exhibits exploring science and technology.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits that make learning fun for children of all ages.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to South Kensington (District, Circle, Piccadilly lines)
transport.tip: Wonderlab is worth the extra cost for a hands-on experience.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.price: Included with entry to the ship
activities.description: Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship. Discover facts about Cutty Sark's voyages and unscramble a secret nautical code to claim a chocolatey treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines history with an engaging activity, and a sweet reward.

transport.tip: Suitable for young explorers age five+ and their families

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Families can enjoy informal Easter egg hunts, picnics, or just a relaxing walk through this inner-city green space.

activities.title: Holland Park Adventure Playground
activities.description: Aimed at older kids (5+), with climbing structures, slides, and more.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a challenging and fun play environment for older toddlers and young children.

transport.tip: Also a smaller toddler playground in Holland Park

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A pirate ship-themed playground inspired by Peter Pan.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a sensory trail, teepees, and plenty of seating for parents.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Notting Hill Gate, then walk

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated entirely to children, with an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool, and a small city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of activities for young children in a central location.

transport.tip: Adults only allowed if accompanying a child.

activities.title: Laaaunch! at Polka Theatre
activities.description: The multi-sensory space-themed Laaaunch!
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for babies

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: Giant Easter egg trail, meet the Easter Bunny, indoor playbarn, Easter entertainment with the Hobblers, outdoor adventure play villages, marshmallow toasting, bubble play, zoo and farm animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Wide range of activities to suit all interests.

activities.title: Paddington The Musical
activities.description: The musical adaptation of the beloved Paddington Bear stories.
activities.whyItWorks: Celebrates British culture, familiar story.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Covent Garden or Charing Cross

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail
activities.description: Discover illustrated animals nestled amongst the blooming habitats of the reserve, identify the letter on each one and complete the secret word in the activity booklet.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor exploration and learning.

activities.title: Easter Quest at Eltham Palace and Gardens
activities.price: Paid, in addition to paid admission
activities.description: Embark on a fun-filled adventure quest around the house and gardens. Follow the Easter trail, hunt for eggs and answer questions along the way to earn a delicious chocolate reward.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun adventure and chocolate reward.

activities.title: My Neighbour Totoro
activities.description: The Royal Shakespeare Company's adaptation of the Studio Ghibli film with elaborate sets and puppetry.
activities.whyItWorks: Gentle, beautiful, and works wonderfully for kids who might find louder, flashier musicals a bit much.

activities.title: Kew Gardens – Sounds of Blossom
activities.description: Outdoor Spring Highlight
activities.whyItWorks: Great outdoor space and sensory experience.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt – Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat
activities.whyItWorks: Motivates kids to walk, outdoor activity.
